perturbation.original_text: Holders whose First Tranche Series E Debt Securities and Second Tranche Series E Debt Securities are remarketed pursuant to this Remarketing Agreement will not otherwise be responsible for the payment of any Remarketing Fee in connection therewith.
perturbation.changed_text: Holders whose First Tranche Series E Debt Securities and Second Tranche Series E Debt Securities are remarketed pursuant to this Remarketing Agreement may be responsible for the payment of a Remarketing Fee in connection therewith, as determined by the Remarketing Agents.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that holders of remarketed securities 'will not otherwise be responsible' for remarketing fees. The modified version introduces uncertainty by stating they 'may be responsible' for the fees, depending on the Remarketing Agents' determination. This directly contradicts the original guarantee and creates ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: On the Remarketing Settlement Date, the Remarketing Agents shall deduct the Remarketing Fee from the gross proceeds of the Remarketing and shall remit any proceeds remaining after such deduction as follows: (i) to the extent such net proceeds relate to Remarketed Securities that form part of any Normal Common Equity Unit (as defined in the Stock Purchase Contract Agreement), to the Securities Intermediary and (ii) to the extent such net proceeds relate to the Separate Securities that were included in the Remarketing as Remarketed Securities to, or at the direction of, the Custodial Agent for payment to the holders of such Separate Securities. Holders whose First Tranche Series E Debt Securities and Second Tranche Series E Debt Securities are remarketed pursuant to this Remarketing Agreement may be responsible for the payment of a Remarketing Fee in connection therewith, as determined by the Remarketing Agents.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text guarantees that holders will not be responsible for remarketing fees, while the changed text states they 'may be responsible,' depending on the Remarketing Agents' decision. This introduces uncertainty and directly contradicts the original guarantee, causing a conflict with the contradicted text. The contradicted text uses the changed language of "Holders whose First Tranche Series E Debt Securities and Second Tranche Series E Debt Securities are remarketed pursuant to this Remarketing Agreement may be responsible for the payment of a Remarketing Fee in connection therewith, as determined by the Remarketing Agents." This reinforces the ambiguity and creates a situation where it is unclear if the Remarketing Fee will be deducted from the gross proceeds and remitted to the Securities Intermediary/Custodial Agent, or if the holders are separately responsible.

perturbation.original_text: Each Remarketing Agent acknowledges, represents and agrees and each additional Remarketing Agent appointed pursuant to Section 8 of this Remarketing Agreement will be required to acknowledge, represent and agree that it has not remarketed or delivered and it will not remarket or deliver, any of the Remarketed Securities, in or from any jurisdiction except under circumstances that are reasonably designed to result in compliance with the applicable securities laws and regulations thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Remarketing Agent acknowledges, represents and agrees and each additional Remarketing Agent appointed pursuant to Section 8 of this Remarketing Agreement will be required to acknowledge, represent and agree that it may remarket or deliver, any of the Remarketed Securities, in or from any jurisdiction regardless of circumstances designed to result in compliance with the applicable securities laws and regulations thereof, as long as they notify MetLife of their decision.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires the Remarketing Agents to comply with applicable securities laws. The modified version creates a contradiction by allowing them to remarket or deliver securities 'regardless of circumstances designed to result in compliance,' provided they notify MetLife. This removes the legally binding obligation to adhere to securities laws, creating a direct conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Remarketing Agent acknowledges, represents and agrees and each additional Remarketing Agent appointed pursuant to Section 8 of this Remarketing Agreement will be required to acknowledge, represent and agree that it may remarket or deliver, any of the Remarketed Securities, in or from any jurisdiction regardless of circumstances designed to result in compliance with the applicable securities laws and regulations thereof, as long as they notify MetLife of their decision. In particular, each Remarketing Agent acknowledges, represents and agrees and each additional Remarketing Agent appointed pursuant to Section 8 of this Remarketing Agreement will be required to acknowledge, represent and agree as set forth in Annex V to this Remarketing Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the principle of compliance with securities laws outlined in the original text. The original text mandates compliance ('will not remarket or deliver...except under circumstances that are reasonably designed to result in compliance'). The changed text permits remarketing 'regardless of circumstances designed to result in compliance,' as long as MetLife is notified. The contradicted text echoes the changed text ('may remarket or deliver, any of the Remarketed Securities, in or from any jurisdiction regardless of circumstances designed to result in compliance with the applicable securities laws and regulations thereof, as long as they notify MetLife of their decision.'). This creates an in-text contradiction because the requirement for compliance with securities laws is effectively nullified, leading to a direct conflict in the obligations of the Remarketing Agents. This is a strong contradiction due to the central importance of regulatory compliance.

perturbation.original_text: This Remarketing Agreement may not be amended or modified except by an instrument in writing signed by, or on behalf of, each of the parties hereto; provided, however, that in the event that the Securities to be remarketed, or any portion thereof, are modified in such a way so that they no longer require registration under the Act, the Company, the Remarketing Agents and the Stock Purchase Contract Agent hereby agree to promptly enter into an amendment to this Remarketing Agreement containing such representations, warranties and covenants as would normally be required to more accurately reflect an offering of unregistered securities.
perturbation.changed_text: This Remarketing Agreement may be amended or modified orally by any authorized representative of any party hereto; provided, however, that in the event that the Securities to be remarketed, or any portion thereof, are modified in such a way so that they no longer require registration under the Act, the Company, the Remarketing Agents and the Stock Purchase Contract Agent hereby agree to promptly enter into an amendment to this Remarketing Agreement containing such representations, warranties and covenants as would normally be required to more accurately reflect an offering of unregistered securities.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly requires amendments to be in writing and signed by all parties. The modified version introduces an in-text contradiction by stating the agreement can be amended 'orally by any authorized representative of any party hereto,' directly negating the original requirement for written agreements. This creates confusion on how amendments are enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Remarketing Agreement may be amended or modified orally by any authorized representative of any party hereto; provided, however, that in the event that the Securities to be remarketed, or any portion thereof, are modified in such a way so that they no longer require registration under the Act, the Company, the Remarketing Agents and the Stock Purchase Contract Agent hereby agree to promptly enter into an amendment to this Remarketing Agreement containing such representations, warranties and covenants as would normally be required to more accurately reflect an offering of unregistered securities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text requires amendments to be in writing and signed. The changed text states amendments can be made orally, directly contradicting the initial requirement. The contradiction is within the same text, as the initial clause is replaced with the contradictory clause, making it a clear and strong in-text contradiction.
